latex: \begin{aligned}\mathbb P[Y=(y_1,\ldots,y_d)\mid X=x]&=\mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid X=x]\prod_{i=2}^{d}\mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x],\\ \mathbb P[Y_1=y_1\mid X=x]&=g_\theta^1(x,y_1),\\ \mathbb P[Y_i=y_i\mid Y_{1:i-1}=y_{1:i-1},X=x]&=g_\theta^i(x,y_{1:i-1},y_i).\end{aligned}
note: Corrected the undefined Y_0 prefix to the one-based preceding-coordinate slice Y_{1:i-1} throughout equation (12).

evidence: The random vector is defined immediately above as Y=(Y_1,...,Y_d), with no Y_0 component.
The chain rule at factor i must condition on the already generated components Y_1 through Y_(i-1).
The printed zero-based slice is inconsistent with both the one-based vector definition and the product starting at i=2.
